mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat despite positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month period signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.3 percentage points in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 1.5% and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues to show month-over-month growth above 0.2%, it indicates persistent inflationary pressures requiring aggressive monetary tightening, which dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: inflationary pressure with tightening monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met, or the Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signifies renewed consumer and business confidence, stimulating construction activity and broader economic growth, which supports equity market appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts show strong growth during identified recovery periods, but SPX 60-day forward returns are flat or negative in more than 60% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating robust corporate output and efficiency, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive short-term market sentiment for S&P 500 over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) by 0.5% or more within a three-month period signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, leading to higher borrowing costs, reduced corporate investment, and negative equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.5% or more in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a rapid FEDFUNDS hike of 0.5% or more within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) exceeding 0.1% month-over-month combined with robust GDP expansion (GDPC1) above 0.5% quarter-over-quarter indicates a healthy and growing economy, providing a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.1% MoM AND GDPC1 growth > 0.5% QoQ
failure_condition: When both employment and GDP show strong growth as specified, SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market where the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is consistently below 4.0%, companies benefit from strong consumer demand, leading to sustained economic activity and positive short-term equity market performance for the S&P 500 over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is consistently below 4.0% for at least three consecutive months.
